name: Munji
title: Loyal Servant of Imam Mujtaba
shortDescription: The loyal servant of Imam Hasan al-Mujtaba (AS) who, after him, became a constant companion of Imam Hussain (AS) and was martyred in Karbala.
fullStory:
Munji ibn Sahm was one of the loyal servants and retainers of the Ahl al-Bayt. His mother 'Hassaniya' was a maid of Imam Sajjad (AS) or Imam Mujtaba (AS). Munji was a sincere servant during the life of Imam Hasan al-Mujtaba (AS) and, after his martyrdom, entered the service of the Master of Martyrs (AS).

When Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan moved from Medina to Mecca and then towards Karbala, Munji was also present on this perilous journey alongside Imam Hasan's (AS) children. As a close companion of the Prophet's (PBUH) family, he never separated from them for a moment.

On the day of Ashura, although Munji was from the class of servants, his bravery and freedom were evident to all. He stepped forward to help the son of the Prophet (PBUH) and rushed to the battlefield.

Munji fought with full valor in the field. With his consecutive attacks, he made Umar Sa'd's soldiers tremble and spared no effort in defending the sanctuary of the Ahl al-Bayt.

Finally, after an unequal battle with the Kufan army, this loyal servant was martyred by 'Hassan ibn Bakr al-Hanzali'. Munji's sacrifice showed that love for the Ahl al-Bayt is not limited to lineage or race, and any heart that is a place of light will shine in Karbala.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى مُنْجِحٍ مَوْلى الحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Munji, the servant of Hussain ibn Ali.
